name: Joanne Brackeen
description: American jazz pianist and music educator
abstract: Joanne Brackeen (born Joanne Grogan; July 26, 1938) is an American jazz pianist and music educator.

interviews.title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Bill Evans-A Tribute

works.t: Swingin’ the Dream: Darn that Dream
Jubilee: Just One of Those Things

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: tour that is called “100 Gold Fingers,” it features ten piano players — ... Joanne Brackeen ... and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Gourse
about_quotes.evidence: A girlfriend last night, Joanne Brackeen plays piano and told me...
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Gourse interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: George Cables, JoAnne Brackeen, Jimmy Garrison, [Charlie] Haden, Larry Willis. Everybody came through, because what happened is, we took a gig at a club called La Boheme.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t think of myself as having a proper apprenticeship, like for instance Barbara Carroll, and JoAnne Brackeen, who played with Stan Getz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I think Joanne Brackeen wrote some very challenging music for Eddie Gomez.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And I was in it and the Maria Schneider big band and Joanne Brackeen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997
